commit.message:
ci: rewrite deploy workflow using official fastapi cloud pattern

The CLI reads FASTAPI_CLOUD_TOKEN and FASTAPI_CLOUD_APP_ID from env
vars directly (via Identity._get_token_from_env). No auth.json writing,
no login step, no cloud.json needed.

Uses astral-sh/setup-uv@v7 and actions/checkout@v5 (also fixes the
Node.js 20 deprecation warnings).

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

repository.name: LingoSummarApi
repository.description: FastAPI backend for LingoSummar — intelligent text summarization using fuzzy logic, K-means clustering, and TF-IDF. PostgreSQL, Alembic, Docker, deployed on FastAPI Cloud.

commit.message:
ci: fix auth by writing auth.json instead of broken --token flag

fastapi cloud login has no --token option. Instead write the PAT
directly into ~/.local/share/fastapi-cli/auth.json (Linux XDG path),
matching how the CLI stores credentials locally.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
fix: allow optional percentage in summarize schemas (#2)

* fix: allow optional percentage in summarize schemas

SummarizeRequest and SummarizeAgainRequest had `percentage: int` with
`ge=30, le=80`, which rejected `0` or `null` from the frontend with a
422 Unprocessable Entity.

Changed both to `int | None` with `ge=1, le=100` so callers can omit
or null-out the field; `_get_percentage()` already handles the
default fallback to a random 30-80 value.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix: widen percentage range to 1-100 across models and simplify fallback

- Text model: percentage ge=30,le=80 → ge=1,le=100
- Summary model: same range update
- _get_percentage: replace random 30-80 fallback with deterministic
  default of 50%; respect user's explicit choice via schema validation
- Remove unused `random` import

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* ci: add GitHub Actions workflow to auto-deploy to FastAPI Cloud

Triggers on every push to main. Installs fastapi-cli, restores the
auth token from the FASTAPI_CLOUD_AUTH GitHub secret, then runs
`fastapi deploy . --no-wait` which uses the app_id from .fastapicloud/cloud.json.

Setup required:
1. Copy the contents of ~/.fastapi/auth.json on your local machine
2. Add it as a GitHub secret named FASTAPI_CLOUD_AUTH in the repo settings

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* ci: fix deploy workflow to use uv + PAT token auth

- Use uv sync to install deps (matches project toolchain)
- Authenticate with `fastapi cloud login --token` using PAT
- Remove incorrect auth.json approach
- Run fastapi via `uv run` to use project venv

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* ci: recreate .fastapicloud/cloud.json in CI before deploy

Since .fastapicloud is gitignored, the folder won't exist after
checkout. Recreate cloud.json from GitHub secrets so `fastapi deploy .`
can find the app_id and team_id.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

---------

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
Document cluster node health feature (#6502)

Adds documentation for the node health status feature implemented in PRs #4758 and #4767,
which enables Valkey-compatible cluster node state tracking.

Important: Node health is passive metadata provided by the cluster manager (control plane) via DFLYCLUSTER CONFIG.
Dragonfly nodes do not actively determine their own health status.

Co-authored-by: copilot-swe-agent[bot] <198982749+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
fix(deps): add en-core-web-sm as explicit dependency for cloud deployment

spaCy model was installed via a separate Dockerfile RUN step, which
doesn't apply when FastAPI Cloud runs uv sync from pyproject.toml
directly. Adding the model as a direct URL dependency ensures it is
included in every environment via uv sync.

- Add en-core-web-sm 3.8.0 wheel URL to pyproject.toml dependencies
- Regenerate uv.lock

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
fix(config): resolve CORS_ORIGINS SettingsError on startup

pydantic-settings v2 calls json.loads() on list[str] fields before
field_validator runs, causing a SettingsError if the env var is not
valid JSON (e.g. plain URL or comma-separated string in a dashboard).

- Change CORS_ORIGINS type from list[str] to str to bypass pydantic-
  settings' complex-type JSON pre-parsing
- Replace field_validator with a @computed_field property cors_origins
  that accepts JSON arrays, comma-separated values, or a single URL
- Update main.py to use settings.cors_origins (the parsed list)
- Update .env.example to document both accepted formats

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
refactor(api): migrate backend from Flask to FastAPI

- Remove Flask app structure: routes.py, models.py, config.py, run.py, requirements.txt
- Add FastAPI app with app/main.py, app/config.py, app/database.py
- Add SQLAlchemy models in app/models/ (text.py, summary.py)
- Add Pydantic schemas in app/schemas/ (text.py, summary.py)
- Add service layer in app/services/ (text_service.py, summary_service.py)
- Add API endpoints in app/api/endpoints/ (summarize.py, texts.py, health.py)
- Add Alembic migrations with initial tables and timezone-aware timestamps
- Add Dockerfile and docker-compose.yml for containerised deployment
- Add pyproject.toml and uv.lock replacing requirements.txt
- Refactor summarizer core: engine.py, helpers.py, models.py replacing legacy files
- Add FastAPI Cloud config via .fastapicloud/cloud.json
- Add comprehensive test suite: test_health, test_summarize, test_texts with conftest

commit.message:
feat(control-plane): implement artifact and review packet retrieval endpoints

Add REST endpoints for retrieving task artifacts, review history,
review cycle packets, parsed packet content, and merge details.

Endpoints:
- GET /tasks/:taskId/artifacts - artifact tree organized by type
- GET /tasks/:taskId/packets/:packetId - parsed JSON packet content
- GET /tasks/:taskId/reviews - review cycle history with decisions
- GET /tasks/:taskId/reviews/:cycleId/packets - specialist packets
- GET /tasks/:taskId/merge - merge queue item + validation runs

New files:
- review/artifacts.controller.ts + artifacts.service.ts
- review/reviews.controller.ts + reviews.service.ts
- merge/merge-details.controller.ts + merge-details.service.ts
- 6 test files with 31 new tests (3,268 total passing)

Closes T084.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
Fix nationality not updating currency display when restoring form from params URL (#7)

* Initial plan

* Add support for individual player URL parameters (goalkeeping, playmaking, etc.)

Co-authored-by: tagliala <556268+tagliala@users.noreply.github.com>

* Remove accidentally committed package-lock.json

Co-authored-by: tagliala <556268+tagliala@users.noreply.github.com>

* Fix fillForm() to update WageWizard.LeagueDetails after restoring league from params URL

Co-authored-by: tagliala <556268+tagliala@users.noreply.github.com>

* Include nation in CHPP link; sync per-team league

- Add LeagueID to each team's array in getTeamDetails so multi-team
  users carry the correct league for each team
- In updateCHPPPlayerFields, set WageWizard_League select value and
  WageWizard.LeagueDetails from the active team's LeagueID so that
  formSerialize() captures the correct nation in the generated link,
  even though the league select is hidden in CHPP mode

* test

* Populate league select before setting CHPP nation

createCountryDropbox() was only called in non-CHPP mode, leaving
the WageWizard_League select with no <option> elements in CHPP
mode. Setting .value on an empty select is a no-op, so the team's
country ID was never actually stored in the field and was missing
from the serialized link. Call createCountryDropbox() in the
CHPP OK path before setupCHPPPlayerFields so the select has
options when updateCHPPPlayerFields sets its value.

* Remove named URL params recognition

Drop fillFormFromPlayerParams and PLAYER_URL_PARAMS. The named
?goalkeeping=X&age=Y&nationality=Z parameter scheme is no longer
needed; the standard ?params= serialized format is the only
supported way to pre-fill the form from a URL.

---------

Co-authored-by: copilot-swe-agent[bot] <198982749+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>
Co-authored-by: tagliala <556268+tagliala@users.noreply.github.com>
Co-authored-by: Geremia Taglialatela <tagliala.dev@gmail.com>

Fix profile workdir: use local repo for Copilot boot, skip git ops

commit.message:
feat(randutil): add random value generation utilities (#607)

New package with 11 random utility functions:
- Int, Float64, Bool: basic random values
- Pick[T]: random element from slice
- Shuffle[T]: random permutation
- Sample[T]: random subset without replacement
- String, Hex, Alpha, AlphaNum: random string generators
- WeightedPick[T]: weighted random selection

All generic functions use math/rand/v2 for performance.
Includes doc.go, 25+ unit tests, 3 benchmarks, 3 examples.

Co-authored-by: Jordan <jordan@users.noreply.github.com>
Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

feat(application): implement audit event query service (T074)

Add AuditQueryService with getEntityTimeline() and searchAuditEvents()
for querying the append-only audit log with flexible, combinable filters
and offset-based pagination.

- New AuditEventQueryPort (read-side, CQRS-lite separation from write port)
- AuditEventFilters: entityType, entityId, eventType, actorType, actorId, startTime, endTime
- PaginatedAuditResult with total count and page metadata
- Input validation (non-empty entity params, time range consistency)
- Pagination normalization (clamping, defaults)
- Infrastructure: findByEntityPaginated() and searchFiltered() with Drizzle dynamic queries
- Infrastructure: createAuditEventQueryPortAdapter() with shared mapRowToRecord()
- 26 unit tests covering timelines, filters, pagination, validation, edge cases

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

test(discovery): add fuzz tests for registry operations (#606)

Add four fuzz targets:
- FuzzRegister: arbitrary service/ID/addr with sentinel error verification
- FuzzResolve: resolution with arbitrary service names
- FuzzSetStatus: status transitions with arbitrary inputs
- FuzzHeartbeat: heartbeat with arbitrary service/ID pairs

Co-authored-by: Jordan <jordan@users.noreply.github.com>
Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
feat: implement Azure Incident Copilot MVP

Complete TypeScript monorepo with all 87 tasks across 9 phases:

- Shared package: types, validation, SQLite persistence (node:sqlite),
  log/job/result repositories, mock AI analysis adapter, seed data
- API server: Express REST API with incident CRUD, log submission,
  job tracking, analysis results, correlation IDs, CORS
- Worker: polling-based async processor with mock AI analysis
- Frontend: React + Vite with incident list, create, detail pages,
  log submission, analysis display, job status polling
- Tests: 67 tests (unit + API integration + E2E workflow)
- Scripts: dev.sh (concurrent startup), verify.sh (E2E verification)

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

feat(cond): add generic conditional and comparison utilities (#513)

New package with 10 generic utility functions:
- Ternary[T]: inline conditional expression
- Coalesce[T], CoalesceFunc[T]: first non-zero value
- Clamp[T]: restrict to range
- Min[T], Max[T]: variadic min/max
- Zero[T], IsZero[T]: zero value helpers
- Switch[T]: pattern matching with Case structs

All functions are generic and type-safe.
Includes doc.go, 25+ unit tests, 3 benchmarks, 4 examples.

Co-authored-by: Jordan <jordan@users.noreply.github.com>
Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

feat(encoding): add base62 encode/decode utilities (#506)

Add a new encoding package with base62 encoding for compact, URL-safe
representations of binary data and integers.

New functions:
- Base62Encode — encode bytes to base62 string
- Base62Decode — decode base62 string to bytes
- Base62EncodeUint64 — encode uint64 to compact base62
- Base62DecodeUint64 — decode base62 string to uint64

Includes 14 tests, 2 benchmarks, and 2 fuzz targets.
100% statement coverage.

Co-authored-by: Jordan <jordan@users.noreply.github.com>
Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

feat(application): implement rework loop with rejection context (T062)

Add ReworkContextService that assembles a validated RejectionContext from
persisted review data (LeadReviewDecision + specialist ReviewPackets +
DevResultPacket) for inclusion in rework TaskPackets.

New files:
- ports/rework-context.ports.ts — Port interfaces for querying rejected
  review data
- services/rework-context.service.ts — Service that builds RejectionContext
  at query time from already-persisted data
- services/rework-context.service.test.ts — 22 tests covering happy path,
  fallbacks, error handling, and defensive parsing

Design: RejectionContext is derived from existing records rather than stored
redundantly, keeping data normalized and avoiding schema migrations.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

docs: refresh business roadmap reality check

- update roadmap with current-head Playwright workflow evidence
- refresh Playwright blocker counts and stale documentation notes
- roll forward roadmap review dates after fresh build and unit validation

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

feat(convert): add safe type conversion utilities (#502)

New package with 13 conversion functions:
- ToInt, ToInt64, ToFloat64, ToUint: string-to-number with fallback
- ToBool: flexible boolean parsing (true/1/yes/on)
- ToString: any-to-string conversion
- ToDuration: string-to-Duration with fallback
- MustInt, MustFloat64: panic-on-error conversions
- Ptr[T]: create pointer to value
- Deref[T], DerefOr[T]: safe pointer dereferencing

All functions return sensible defaults instead of errors.
Includes doc.go, 25+ unit tests, 3 benchmarks, 4 examples.

Co-authored-by: Jordan <jordan@users.noreply.github.com>

feat(stringutil): add string utility functions (#496)

New package with 8 string utilities:
- ToKebabCase, ToPascalCase: case conversion
- PadLeft, PadRight: string padding
- IsBlank: empty/whitespace check
- Reverse: rune-aware string reversal
- WordWrap: line wrapping at word boundaries
- CountWords: word counting

All functions are Unicode-aware (rune-based, not byte-based).
Includes doc.go, 20+ unit tests, 3 benchmarks, 1 fuzz, 3+ examples.

feat(maputil): add generic map utility functions (#487)

New package with 9 generic map utilities:
- Keys, Values: extract keys/values as slices
- Merge: combine multiple maps (last-write-wins)
- Pick, Omit: select/exclude specific keys
- Filter: predicate-based filtering
- MapValues: transform values preserving keys
- Invert: swap keys and values
- Equal: deep equality check

All functions are nil-safe and non-mutating.
Includes doc.go, 22 unit tests, 3 benchmarks, 4 examples.
README updated with package entry.

commit.message:
feat: implement Phase 11 - Error Handling and Resilience

- Add structured AppError type with codes, HTTP mapping, and error wrapping
- Add circuit breaker pattern (CLOSED/OPEN/HALF_OPEN state machine)
- Add webhook-based alert mechanism (fire-and-forget, no-op when unconfigured)
- Add enhanced logger with WithContext for operation/component/instance prefixing
- Add jitter to exponential backoff retry in HTTP client
- Add automatic cleanup on backup failure (move to incomplete + alert)
- Add stuck backup detection in scheduler with configurable timeout
- Wire alerter to orchestrator and scheduler via config and dependency injection
- Update API handlers and middleware to use structured AppError responses
- Add new config options: ALERT_WEBHOOK_URL, BACKUP_STUCK_TIMEOUT_MINUTES
- Add comprehensive tests for all new components (40 new tests)

commit.message:
Add sarma review and profile commands

- New lib/profile.ps1: reviewer profile generation from ADO PR history
  - Fetches PRs where reviewer was active, extracts code comments
  - Categorizes by focus area (dead code, edge cases, logging, tests, etc.)
  - Generates structured profile with examples and style analysis
  - Stores/retrieves profiles from Azure Blob Storage

- sarma profile <alias>: build a reviewer's style profile
  - Fetches up to 30 recent PRs, extracts code review comments
  - Auto-categorizes and generates review persona document
  - --show: display existing profile
  - --refresh: regenerate profile

- sarma review <PR> [--alias <reviewer>]: review a PR
  - Without --alias: generic code review
  - With --alias: loads reviewer profile, reviews as that persona
  - Creates comment threads on the PR via ADO MCP

- Worker updated: handles 'review' task type (no branch, copilot-driven)

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Add minimum heart rate and explicit labels to lap analysis

Enhanced analyze_activity lap display:

- Heart rate now shows: min:X/avg:Y/max:Z bpm with explicit labels
- Handles cases where Strava lap data lacks min_heartrate field
- Falls back to stream-based calculation for complete HR analysis

Example output:

  After:  ❤️ min:140/avg:150/max:165 bpm

is available even for older activities.

commit.message:
Add sarma reserve/release — block Dev Box for manual PR work

reserve <PR-number>:
- Agent checks out PR branch, cleans build artifacts, opens solution
- Worker enters reserved state (blocks future tasks)
- Worker table shows reserved=true, reservedPR=<number>

release (run on Dev Box):
- .\sarma-worker.ps1 release
- Creates .sarma-release file, worker resumes polling

Flow: reserve → agent sets up env → worker blocks → you work manually →
      release → worker unblocks → ready for next task

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Exclude .sarma* files via .git/info/exclude (no .gitignore edit)

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Add sarma revise command — address PR review comments

Usage:
  .\sarma.ps1 revise 1993956              # address all comments
  .\sarma.ps1 revise 1993956 --just-me    # only your comments

How it works:
- Submits a 'revise' task type with PR number
- Worker checks out default branch (copilot handles PR branch via MCP)
- Prompt tells copilot to: fetch PR comments, make fixes, commit, push
- Copilot uses its ADO MCP to read threads and reply when resolved
- PR auto-updates since it pushes to the same branch
- .sarma-done signals completion

Added 'revise' to default worker task types.

commit.message:
[DescriptorLoadOp] Fuse transpose into descriptor load for dot operands (#6238)

Add FuseTransWithDescriptorLoad to the OptimizeDotOperands pass, which
fuses tt.trans into tt.descriptor_load when the transpose result feeds a
dot operation. Instead of reversing the MakeTensorDescOp parameters
(which would violate the row-major invariant), the fusion keeps the
descriptor unchanged and produces a DescriptorLoadOp with a transposed
result type, tagged with block_io = "column_major".

In the LLVM lowering, teach DescriptorLoadOpToBlockIOConversion to
handle column_major descriptors: extract surface parameters in the
descriptor's natural row-major order and swap indices to align with the
result type's coordinate system, generating transposed 2D block loads.

---------

Signed-off-by: Tiotto, Ettore <ettore.tiotto@intel.com>
Co-authored-by: Copilot <175728472+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Update architecture flow diagrams: Orchestrator as central controller

Both the overview and full data-flow diagrams now clearly show the
Orchestrator as the top-level controller that reaches out to both
VisionStreamProcessor and HippocampalComplex, rather than implying
a chain where VSP calls into HC.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Covers:

  RecognisedVisualEntity, Sensation_Location, Graph quad-tree), key methods

commit.message:
Fix: rapid-fire Ctrl+C (500ms apart) + exit command to close window

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Fix: wait 10s after .sarma-done before Ctrl+C, close window, fix dict merge

- Wait 10s after .sarma-done detected (copilot still executing echo command)
- Remove -NoExit so window closes after Ctrl+C
- Fix duplicate 'status' key crash in Update-TaskStatus (use foreach merge)
- Remove redundant status assignment in sessionFields

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Fix TestApicalReinforcementDoesNotMatchSimilarPatterns: remove overlapping positions

Root cause: B1/B2 test patterns shared SOM columns (100,0) and (200,0) with
trained PatternA steps. The SOM's AllTimeSupportedLabels fallback causes burst-
fired trained columns to contribute 'PatternA' to cyclePredictions. With only
one trained label this immediately sets intersect.Count=1 => DONE mode =>
subsequent Fire() throws InvalidOperationException.

Fix: redesign all 3 B-patterns to use entirely non-overlapping SOM columns:
  B1: X=400-640 (same 10-unit spacing, different location)
  B2: X=150-390 (fills gaps between PatternA's trained bands)
  B3: X=700-940 (distant region)
Untrained columns produce empty cycle-predictions so no DONE mode is triggered.
Updated docstrings to explain the column-specific prediction semantics.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
fix(security): SEC-003 exception hardening — 579 broad catches addressed

Standing on Giants:
- Deming (PDCA, 1950): systematic defect elimination
- Shannon (SNR, 1948): noise reduction in error handling
- OWASP (CWE-396): overly broad catch blocks

SEC-003 Exception Hardening across 185 files in core/:
- 276 catches narrowed to specific types (SEC-003 tagged)
  • OSError for I/O, network, file operations
  • ValueError for data parsing, validation
  • RuntimeError for engine/pipeline boundaries
  • ImportError for optional dependency loading
  • ConnectionError for network/WebSocket
  • asyncio.CancelledError for async boundaries
  • json.JSONDecodeError for JSON parsing
  • KeyError for dict access boundaries
- 305 catches annotated as legitimate boundaries (BLE001 tagged)
  • Outer pipeline execution boundaries (re-log + propagate)
  • Handler dispatch boundaries (unknown handler errors)
  • Campaign/benchmark execution boundaries

Key files hardened:
- core/bridges/rust_lifecycle.py (13→0 broad catches)
- core/sovereign/apex_engine.py (10→2 annotated boundaries)
- core/spearpoint/benchmark_dominance.py (9→5 annotated boundaries)
- core/sovereign/spearpoint_pipeline.py (9→0 broad catches)
- core/sat/ledger_gate.py (9→0 broad catches)
- core/pek/kernel.py (9→1 remaining boundary)
- core/pat/bridge.py (7→2 annotated boundaries)
+ 170 additional files

Verification:
- 556/556 Python files compile clean (py_compile)
- 0 ruff BLE001 violations (was 4 tuple-catches with redundant Exception)
- 25 pre-existing F821 (forward refs) unchanged
- No new lint errors introduced

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>
